channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Orders Tolls and Blockade as Fighting With Iran Resumes
item.description: President Trump notified Congress that he had resumed strikes on Iran, as the cease-fire effectively collapsed. He also ordered a naval blockade and announced shipping tolls in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang

media:content.media:description: Boats anchored off Oman’s northern Musandam Peninsula near the Strait of Hormuz last month.

item.title: New York Is Having a Legionnaires’ Outbreak. Here’s What to Know.
item.description: Health officials had not identified the source of the bacteria but said they were optimistic about finding a culprit.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Goldstein

media:content.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Guggenheim Museum was one of 31 buildings that tested positive for the bacteria that causes Legionnaires’ disease. The museum said a sample came from an external water cooling tower not accessible to the public

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 14, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Jon Stewart Mocks Mitch McConnell’s Recovery Photo
item.description: The “Daily Show” host called the image “totally believable evidence that Mitch McConnell is not only alive, but the happiest boy in the hospital.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: La foca más famosa de Australia es un fenómeno viral que preocupa a los expertos
item.description: Neil, un elefante marino de más de 1000 kilos, visita dos veces al año la costa de Tasmania y se ha convertido en estrella de internet, aunque los especialistas piden mantener la distancia.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

item.title: Ataque de bisonte en Yellowstone: un turista sale volando por los aires
item.description: Un hombre de 65 años fue hospitalizado con múltiples fracturas tras ser embestido por un toro bisonte que deambulaba por un campamento del Parque Nacional de Yellowstone visiblemente agitado.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan

media:content.media:description: A tourist taking photographs of bison in Yellowstone National Park in 2024.

item.title: Así es como Putin hizo de Japón un centro de espionaje
item.description: Desde un rascacielos de Tokio, una unidad de inteligencia militar localiza el equipo de alta tecnología que Rusia necesita para librar la guerra.
item.dc:creator: Jane Bradley, Michael Schwirtz and Adam Goldman

media:content.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: La oficina de Aeroflot en la Torre Toranomon Kotohira de Tokio. Las débiles leyes de espionaje de Japón y su floreciente industria de alta tecnología lo han convertido en una base crucial para el esfuerzo bélico ruso.

item.title: The Ukraine-Russia War Reaches a New Phase
item.description: The ground war in Ukraine has stalemated and the crucial fight has shifted to the skies — missiles and interceptors.
item.dc:creator: Serge Schmemann

item.title: Protests Break Out After ICE Agent Kills Person in Maine
item.description: People took to the streets to protest after a federal agent fatally shot a person during a vehicle stop in Biddeford, Maine, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal and Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Some chanted “ICE out of Maine” at a rally and vigil after the ICE killing of a person Monday morning in Biddeford.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Some New York City Parks Leave the Light on for Soccer Camaraderie
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, July 14, 2026.

item.title: Los robots ucranianos revolucionan la guerra terrestre
item.description: Empezaron como vehículos de transporte de materiales. Ahora, los robots terrestres evacuan heridos, defienden trincheras e incluso matan.
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova and Paul Mozur

media:content.media:description: Entrenamiento con un vehículo no tripulado, en la región de Donbás, al este de Ucrania, en 2025. Los batallones de robots terrestres llevan a cabo ahora miles de misiones cada mes.

item.title: Agitation in Dementia Can Be Helped by Medical Cannabis, Study Suggests
item.description: A combination of THC and CBD eased symptoms in an especially frail population: patients with advanced dementia near the end of their lives.
item.dc:creator: Pam Belluck

media:content.media:credit: Eva Marie Uzcategui for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Emilio Gonzalez, left, participated in a clinical trial of a cannabinoid treatment for agitation in people with advanced dementia. His son, Dennys Gonzalez, right, said it eased his father’s distress and frustration.

item.title: Deportations by India Cause Tension Along Border with Bangladesh
item.description: India is driving out Bangladeshi immigrants it says are in the country illegally. Bangladesh is pushing back, leaving people stranded along the border amid increasing tensions between the countries.
item.dc:creator: Anupreeta Das, Saif Hasnat, Hari Kumar and Atul Loke

media:content.media:description: An Indian border guard patrols the border near Hakimpur in West Bengal last month.

item.title: OpenAI Is Showing Kalshi’s World Cup Odds in ChatGPT
item.description: The partnership, a first of its kind for the artificial intelligence company, will use the prediction market’s data to power some search results about the soccer tournament.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac

media:content.media:credit: Eric Helgas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kalshi has struck a partnership with OpenAI tied to the World Cup.

item.title: Pat Oliphant, Cartoonist Who Skewered the Powerful, Dies at 90
item.description: The winner of countless awards, including a Pulitzer Prize, he was described in 1990 as “the most influential political cartoonist now working.”
item.dc:creator: Robert D. McFadden

media:content.media:description: The political cartoonist Pat Oliphant at work in his studio in Washington in 1998.

item.title: Maine ICE Shooting: What We Know About the Death of Joan Sebastian Guerrero
item.description: A federal immigration agent shot and killed a man in a car on Monday morning in Biddeford, Maine. It was the second fatal encounter in a week involving an agent and a person in a vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Witnesses in Biddeford, Maine, heard gunfire early Monday morning.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 14, 2026
item.description: Gus Bloxham and Bharati Hemmady make their New York Times Crossword debut.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Whatever today’s crossword throws at your brain, trust us: It can handle it.

item.title: Health Dept. Rescinds Freeze on $10 Billion for 5 Democratic States
item.description: The reversal of the funding pause came after the administration faced repeated setbacks in a suit challenging the decision, which could have jeopardized programs for low-income households.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim

media:content.media:description: Last week, the Health and Human Services Department sent letters to five states saying that it was voiding measures to limit the states’ access to federal funding for child care and social services.

item.title: Brote de ciclosporiasis: autoridades de salud señalan a las lechugas como posible fuente
item.description: La enfermedad, que ha afectado a miles de personas en gran parte de Estados Unidos, va camino de convertirse en el mayor brote de la historia reciente.
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan

media:content.media:description: Las autoridades sanitarias de Míchigan dijeron en un comunicado que la lechuga u otros vegetales de hoja verde podrían ser una posible fuente del reciente brote de ciclosporiasis.

item.title: Wildfires Close Popular National Forest Area in Minnesota
item.description: Several fires in northeastern Minnesota are causing dangerous conditions and smoke in the Boundary Waters Canoe Area, which is used for camping and other outdoor recreation.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Mokam and Erin McCann

item.title: Graham’s Death Complicates G.O.P. Agenda in Congress
item.description: Senators returned to the Capitol mourning their colleague, who played pivotal roles on multiple issues confronting lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold and Carl Hulse

media:content.media:description: A lowered flag at the U.S. Capitol on Monday in honor of Senator Lindsey Graham, who died over the weekend.

item.title: Tension Emerges Among Democratic Socialists Over 2028 Endorsement
item.description: A meeting to decide on a plan to back a presidential candidate, most likely Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, turned acrimonious, and a proposal to poll members was rejected.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:description: Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez of New York is widely seen as the best-known democratic socialist who could be a contender for president in 2028.

item.title: Democratic Senate Candidates in Maine Denounce Deadly ICE Shooting
item.description: The killing of a man in Biddeford quickly became an issue in the Senate race, with Democrats aiming to tie Senator Susan Collins to President Trump’s immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner

media:content.media:description: Protesters in Biddeford, Maine, outside Senator Susan Collins’s office after the fatal shooting of a man by an ICE agent in the city on Monday.

item.title: South Carolina Governor Appoints Lindsey Graham’s Sister to Finish Senate Term
item.description: Gov. Henry McMaster of South Carolina appointed Senator Lindsey Graham’s sister, Darline Graham Nordone, to finish his Senate term.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: The Ridiculous Rise of Victor Marx
item.description: Once started, the process of voter radicalization isn’t easy to modulate.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: AAron Ontiveroz, via The Denver Post/Getty Images

item.title: La hermana de Lindsey Graham ocupará el cargo de él en el Senado
item.description: El nombramiento de Darline Graham Nordone ocurrió horas después de que el presidente Donald Trump indicó que la había recomendado para ocupar el puesto de su difunto hermano.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei

media:content.media:description: Darline Graham Nordone said it would be a “privilege to get to finish” some of her brother’s work.

item.title: ‘Lettuce or Salad Greens’ Identified as Potential Source of Cyclospora Outbreak
item.description: Michigan health officials announced the preliminary findings, but they cautioned that other foods could not be ruled out. The illness has sickened thousands across the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan

media:content.media:description: Health officials in Michigan said in a statement that lettuce or salad greens could be a potential source of the recent outbreak of cyclosporiasis.

item.title: The Dangerous Appeal of Cruelty
item.description: When did politics get so cruel? On “The Opinions,” David French argues that we’ve started confusing cruelty with courage, rewarding politicians who break norms and mistake outrage for strength.

item.title: Are Fears of American Gerontocracy Fair?
item.description: Donald Trump. Mitch McConnell. American politics is once again grappling with the realities of aging leadership. On “The Opinions,” David Wallace-Wells argues that the United States has become a gerontocracy — and that as leaders stay in power longer, concerns about their health, transparency and ability to govern become harder to ignore.

item.title: Trump’s Strait of Hormuz Fee Could Double the Cost of Shipping
item.description: The potential expense of a 20 percent charge to move oil and other goods through the strait has stirred concern among shippers.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Two vessels off the coast of Oman last month. President Trump did not explain exactly how the 20 percent fee would be calculated.

item.title: Texas Faces Days and Days of Heavy Rain
item.description: Meteorologists with the National Weather Service called the forecast “bleak” and warned that some spots could record up to 10 inches of rain this week.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones

item.title: Trump Says Fighting With Iran Has Resumed as He Orders Blockade and Tolls
item.description: After several days of strikes, the president notified Congress that fighting had begun again and announced shipping fees that his administration previously deemed illegal.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Boats anchored off Oman’s northern Musandam Peninsula near the Strait of Hormuz last month.

item.title: Un agente del ICE dispara y mata a un hombre que conducía un vehículo en Maine
item.description: Las autoridades estatales investigan lo ocurrido en Biddeford. Es el segundo tiroteo mortal relacionado con agentes migratorios en menos de una semana. No está claro si el conductor era la persona a la que buscaba el ICE.
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin, Heather Beasley Doyle, Miriam Jordan and Hamed Aleaziz

media:content.media:description: Agentes de las fuerzas del orden en el lugar de un tiroteo mortal el lunes en Biddeford, Maine.

item.title: ICE Shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo Through Open Passenger Window in Houston, Witnesses Say
item.description: A congresswoman and a lawyer relayed witness accounts that diverge from Immigration and Customs Enforcement’s version of the fatal encounter.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín, Robin Stein and Devon Lum

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Family members embracing next to a makeshift memorial for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo at the location where he was shot by an ICE agent in Houston.

item.title: Trump Sharply Cuts the Size of Two National Monuments in Utah
item.description: Native American tribes and environmental groups are expected to challenge the move to shrink the Bears Ears and Grand Staircase-Escalante National Monuments.
item.dc:creator: Maxine Joselow

media:content.media:description: A hiker in the Grand Staircase-Escalante National Monument in Utah.

item.title: Helicopter Pilot Dies While Fighting Wildfire in Western Colorado
item.description: The pilot was the only person onboard when his helicopter crashed into a remote reserve during a firefighting mission.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri

media:content.media:credit: Michael Seamans/The Gazette, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Late last month, a helicopter helped douse the Gold Mountain fire near Ouray and Ridgway, Colo.

item.title: Trump Reimposes a Naval Blockade of Iran
item.description: Plus, Lindsey Graham’s sister was appointed to his Senate seat. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: A vessel at the Straight of Hormuz, seen from Oman today.

item.title: The Best Hairbrushes, and How to Clean Them
item.description: Expert advice on choosing — and caring for — one of the hardest-working beauty tools.
item.dc:creator: Kari Molvar

item.title: Corn and Miso Pasta Salad
item.description: The interplay between the crunch of the celery, the chew of the pasta and the pop of the corn kernels makes this (potentially vegan) pasta salad a textural pleasure to eat.

media:content.media:credit: Nico Schinco for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne.

item.title: Butter Mochi
item.description: Tender and chewy, this big-batch dessert — as comforting as cake and as fun as bar cookies — is always a hit at parties.

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Simpson for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.

item.title: A Guide to the Uncanny 2026 Midterm Elections, and How We Got Here
item.description: A lot of things have changed in American politics since our writer went on parental leave.
item.dc:creator: Jess Bidgood
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times

item.title: Europe Hit With Wildfires Amid Heat Waves
item.description: Wildfires have hit countries across Europe, including France and Spain, as heat waves have been thought to be linked to thousands of excess deaths, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

item.title: Lindsey Graham’s Death Transforms the Race for His Democratic Challenger
item.description: Annie Andrews, a pediatrician, has offered condolences to those who loved Mr. Graham while considering the political fallout from his death. Republicans are scrambling to arrange a new primary election.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei

media:content.media:credit: Sean Rayford for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dr. Annie Andrews, a pediatrician, is the Democratic Senate candidate in South Carolina.

item.title: Sam Neill’s Best Movie Performances: Where to Stream
item.description: From his global breakthrough in “Jurassic Park” to the more recent “Peaky Blinders,” the actor’s range was always on display.
item.dc:creator: Maya Salam

item.title: Israel’s Regime-Change Plan for Iran
item.description: Here’s the wild back story behind a failed plan to install Mahmoud Ahmadinejad as Iran’s next leader.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mahmoud Ahmadinejad in 2024.

item.title: Hamas Forces Interfered With Aid Distribution, U.N. Says
item.description: A raid by armed personnel led the World Food Program to suspend its operations in one part of Gaza, the U.N. said. Hamas disputed the accusations.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Adam Rasgon

media:content.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Israel’s war against Hamas has left homes largely destroyed in Khan Younis in southern Gaza.

item.title: Trump Reimposes Naval Blockade, as U.S. and Iran Escalate Strikes
item.description: After several days of attacks, Tehran and Washington have effectively reverted to the state of open conflict that existed before the United States and Iran agreed to a cease-fire in June.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Leo Sands and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Ships sailing near the Strait of Hormuz off the eastern coast of the United Arab Emirates at Khor Fakkan on Monday.

item.title: Ed King, 89, Scarred Veteran of Mississippi’s Civil Rights Struggle, Dies
item.description: A minister disfigured in an attack on his life, he became what one historian called “the most visible white activist in the Mississippi movement.”
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter

media:content.media:description: Ed King in 1970 speaking at a memorial service for James Earl Green, one of the two students killed during a shooting at Jackson State College that May.

item.title: Los científicos encuentran azúcar en el centro de la Vía Láctea
item.description: Es la primera vez que se detecta una molécula de azúcar en el espacio interestelar. El descubrimiento ofrece pistas nuevas y fascinantes pistas sobre cómo pudo haber surgido la vida en la Tierra.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Dzombak

media:content.media:description: El centro galáctico de la Vía Láctea, captado por el telescopio espacial Spitzer en 2006.

item.title: Would a Social Media Ban in the U.S. Be Effective?
item.description: On the “Hard Fork” podcast, the co-host Casey Newton argues that a new law won’t necessarily be ineffective just because adherence to it is low initially. The same could be true if a social media ban were enacted in the United States.
